tools.name: search_oncology
tools.description: Search a keyword across every Cure Cancer With AI dataset at once — research papers, news, blog posts, FDA approvals, and clinical trials — with results grouped by type. Use this first for broad discovery, then fetch a single record by id/slug/nctId for full detail.

tools.name: list_research
tools.description: List peer-reviewed oncology research papers ingested from PubMed (abstracts, authors, journal, plain-language summaries). Filter by cancer type, treatment type, keyword, or publication date.

tools.name: get_research_paper
tools.description: Fetch a single research paper by its internal id or PubMed id.

tools.name: list_news
tools.description: List curated cancer news articles aggregated from trusted sources. Filter by cancer type, keyword, or published date.

tools.name: list_blog_posts
tools.description: List editorial blog articles (excerpts). Use get_blog_post for full content. Filter by category, cancer-type tag, or keyword.

tools.name: get_blog_post
tools.description: Fetch a single blog post by slug, including the full article content.

tools.name: list_fda_approvals
tools.description: List FDA-approved oncology drugs with indication, company, approval date, and label links. Filter by cancer type, keyword, or approval date.

tools.name: list_clinical_trials
tools.description: List clinical trials from public registries (conditions, status, intervention type). Filter by condition, status (e.g. RECRUITING), or keyword.

tools.name: get_clinical_trial
tools.description: Fetch a single clinical trial by NCT id (or internal id), including eligibility criteria and locations.

tools.name: predict_ppi
tools.description: Predict the binding-affinity class for a pair of proteins using the IBM MAMMAL biomedical foundation model. Returns label "1" (interacting) or "0" (non-interacting). Inference is CPU-bound and may take up to ~60s.

tools.name: predict_dti
tools.description: Predict drug–target binding affinity as pKd (−log10 Kd; higher = stronger binding) using IBM MAMMAL. Inference is CPU-bound and may take up to ~60s.

tools.name: predict_clintox
tools.description: Predict clinical-trial toxicity for a compound using IBM MAMMAL. Returns pred 1 (toxic / likely to fail trials) or 0 (not toxic) plus a raw score. Inference is CPU-bound and may take up to ~60s.

tools.name: search_compounds
tools.description: Find pharmaceutical compounds two ways: by example drugs you already know (fuzzy-matched), or by setting target characteristics on a −4…+4 scale. Provide exactly one of `examples` or `preferences`. Use list_compound_characteristics for the available preference names.

tools.name: list_compound_characteristics
tools.description: List the pharmaceutical-compound characteristics (each scored on the −4…+4 scale) that can be used as preference keys in search_compounds.

tools.name: mammal_health
tools.description: Check whether the IBM MAMMAL prediction model is loaded and ready. No API key required. Call this before predict_* tools if a prior prediction timed out.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_oncology, list_blog_posts, get_blog_post · Private data: search_oncology, mammal_health · Public sink: get_blog_post.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_oncology, list_blog_posts, get_blog_post · Private data: search_oncology, mammal_health · Public sink: get_blog_post

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
